theme_and_archetype.description: Deep obsidian backgrounds combined with stark white technical typography and high-visibility safety yellow accents. Designed to feel like a high-end live event command center, communicating raw structural power, precision, and safety.

hierarchy.h1: text-5xl md:text-6xl+ font-black uppercase tracking-tighter leading-none text-white
hierarchy.h2: text-4xl md:text-5xl font-bold uppercase tracking-tight text-white
hierarchy.h3: text-2xl md:text-3xl font-bold uppercase tracking-tight text-white
hierarchy.body: text-base font-normal leading-relaxed text-zinc-400 font-mono tracking-tight
hierarchy.overline: text-xs font-bold uppercase tracking-[0.2em] text-yellow-400 font-mono

typography.rules: Never center headings. Align strictly left to maintain a rigid, blueprint-like grid. Typography should feel engineered.

layout_and_spacing.spacing_scale: Generous scaling. Use p-8 or p-12 inside bordered containers to prevent collision. Major section gaps should be py-24 or py-32.
layout_and_spacing.grid_system: The Control Room Grid (Strict, Dense). Use grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-4 lg:grid-cols-12 with gap-6.
layout_and_spacing.alignment: Strict Left Alignment. Tension through asymmetry. Never auto-center blocks unless explicitly inside a symmetrical 3D overlay.

surfaces_and_borders.cards: Flat solid backgrounds (bg-[#121212]). Sharp edges (rounded-none). No shadows. 1px solid border-zinc-800.
surfaces_and_borders.glassmorphism: BANNED. Use solid opaque surfaces or harsh grid borders to maintain the raw industrial aesthetic. No blurred translucent layers.
surfaces_and_borders.borders: Expose the skeleton. Utilize 'border-collapse' styles to make sections look like technical spreadsheets or rigging blueprints.

scene_setup.camera: PerspectiveCamera positioned low, looking slightly upwards at the stage for a monumental 'hero' angle.

lighting.ambient: Low intensity (0.1) pure white. Keep the environment very dark.
lighting.spotlights: Dynamic SpotLights (Cyan, Magenta, or Pure White) casting down from the trusses. Use penumbra and high intensity to slice through the dark.

objects.stage: Dark grey metallic plane or box with a rough material.
objects.trusses: Build simple grouped geometries (Boxes/Cylinders) mapped to a metallic material to act as overhead lighting trusses. They must be linked to a Y-axis position state to move up/down.
objects.controls: OrbitControls with restricted polar angles (preventing the user from looking under the floor).

ui_overlay.positioning: Absolute positioning over the canvas using HTML overlays.
ui_overlay.control_panel: A floating 'Control Panel' bento box in the bottom corner containing Toggle Switches (Lights On/Off) and Sliders (Truss Height). Connect these UI inputs directly to the React state controlling the 3D scene.

components.buttons: Square/Sharp edges (rounded-none). Primary: bg-yellow-400 text-black hover:bg-yellow-500 font-bold uppercase transition-colors. Secondary: bg-transparent border-2 border-zinc-700 text-white hover:border-white transition-colors.
components.navigation: Fixed header, bg-[#0A0A0A] border-b border-zinc-900. Logo left, Links right + 'Request Quote' Button.
components.forms: Inputs: bg-[#121212] border border-zinc-800 text-white rounded-none focus:border-yellow-400 focus:ring-1 focus:ring-yellow-400 p-4 font-mono.
components.data_testid: MANDATORY. Every button, input, toggle, section, and 3D control panel must have a descriptive kebab-case data-testid attribute.

images.description: Dramatic stage lighting setup, monochrome vibe.
Concert stage lighting truss with crowd.
Dark room with spotlights, live event setup.
Stage curtain hanging closed in dark theater.
Protective helmet and case - industrial safety.

tech_stack.file_type_rules: Use .js and .jsx ONLY. Do NOT use TypeScript (.tsx/.ts) or TypeScript type definitions.

instructions_to_main_agent: Build a single-page interactive landing for Apex Stage Riggers in JS (no TS).
Hero Section MUST be a full-screen or 80vh Canvas using @react-three/fiber and @react-three/drei.
Inside the Canvas, procedurally build a mock 3D stage with 'trusses' (use Box or Cylinder geometries grouped together) and SpotLights attached to them.

Add HTML UI overlay on top of the 3D Canvas (z-index, pointer-events-auto for interactive bits). This UI must act as an 'Engineering Control Panel' with buttons/sliders to raise/lower the 3D truss and toggle lights. Map these inputs to React state variables passed into the Canvas elements.
Implement the remaining sections below the Hero: Services (Bento Grid of technical capabilities), Projects/Gallery (use provided media URLs), About, and a Request a Quote form.
Typography: Use Cabinet Grotesk for headings and IBM Plex Mono for body via standard CDN or generic fonts. Enforce the Dark Theme (Obsidian) + Safety Yellow (#FACC15) Accent.
Use pure sharp edges (rounded-none). Absolutely NO rounded corners for cards/buttons to keep it brutal and technical.
Add 'data-testid' to all interactive elements, forms, and the 3D control panel for reliable testing.
Do NOT use placeholder image URLs. ONLY use the URLs provided in the media_assets object.
